title: Modeling Cycles, Units, Error, and Uncertainty · Model Lens: Units Scales
summary: Evaluate demand and capacity for a public program using incomplete measurements. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math int units scales increment in a validated demand-capacity model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Track units, scales, significant digits, and uncertainty through multi-stage calculations.
Turn stakeholder needs into answerable model questions, quantities, constraints, and validation criteria.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Calculate Units Scales evidence
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 19 units, changes by −6 units, then by +2 units. Calculate the final quantity.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Preserve the signs and compute 19 − 6 + 2; compare with the starting scale.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Preserve the signs and compute 19 − 6 + 2; compare with the starting scale.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units.
